title: Shopping
description: Vocabulary for shopping, prices, and retail interactions

vocabulary.meaning: A reduction in the original price
vocabulary.example: There is a 30% discount on all items today.

vocabulary.meaning: A printed record of a purchase
vocabulary.example: Please keep your receipt for returns.

vocabulary.meaning: Money returned after returning an item
vocabulary.example: I would like a refund for this defective item.

vocabulary.meaning: A room in a store where you try on clothes
vocabulary.example: The fitting rooms are at the back of the store.

vocabulary.meaning: A person who handles payments in a store
vocabulary.example: Please go to any open cashier.

vocabulary.meaning: A period when items are sold at lower prices
vocabulary.example: The end-of-season sale starts this weekend.

vocabulary.meaning: To swap a purchased item for another
vocabulary.example: Can I exchange this for a larger size?

vocabulary.meaning: A basket on wheels used while shopping
vocabulary.example: I'll grab a cart near the entrance.

vocabulary.meaning: An item bought at a lower price than usual
vocabulary.example: This coat was a real bargain at 50% off.

vocabulary.meaning: The place in a store where you pay for items
vocabulary.example: Please proceed to checkout when ready.

vocabulary.meaning: A sale to clear out old stock at reduced prices
vocabulary.example: These shoes are on clearance — 70% off.

vocabulary.meaning: A measurement used for clothing or shoes
vocabulary.example: Do you have this shirt in a large size?

vocabulary.meaning: A named product or company known for its goods
vocabulary.example: Which brand do you prefer?

vocabulary.meaning: A tag attached to a product with information
vocabulary.example: Check the label for washing instructions.

vocabulary.meaning: A machine-readable pattern on a product for pricing
vocabulary.example: The cashier scanned the barcode.

vocabulary.meaning: A line of people waiting to be served
vocabulary.example: There is a long queue at the checkout.

vocabulary.meaning: A card that earns points for purchases at a store
vocabulary.example: Do you have a loyalty card with us?

vocabulary.meaning: A prepaid card used as a gift that can be spent in a store
vocabulary.example: I received a gift card for my birthday.

vocabulary.meaning: A paper or digital token that gives a discount
vocabulary.example: Use this voucher for 20% off your next order.

vocabulary.meaning: Buying goods via the internet
vocabulary.example: Online shopping is very convenient.

vocabulary.meaning: The process of bringing goods to a customer
vocabulary.example: Free delivery on orders over $50.

vocabulary.meaning: Following the delivery progress of an order
vocabulary.example: Use the tracking number to check your parcel.

vocabulary.meaning: Rules about returning purchased items to a store
vocabulary.example: Please read the return policy before buying.

vocabulary.meaning: A guarantee that a product will be repaired or replaced if faulty
vocabulary.example: This laptop comes with a 2-year warranty.

vocabulary.meaning: A formal promise about a product's quality
vocabulary.example: We offer a 30-day money-back guarantee.

vocabulary.meaning: Credit given in place of a cash refund to spend in the store
vocabulary.example: You can use the store credit on your next visit.

vocabulary.meaning: Paying for goods in several regular payments
vocabulary.example: You can pay in monthly installments.

vocabulary.meaning: Purchasing a large quantity of something at once
vocabulary.translation: mua si / mua so luong lon
vocabulary.example: Bulk buying saves money in the long run.

vocabulary.meaning: Selling goods to individual customers in a store
vocabulary.example: The retail price is higher than online.

vocabulary.meaning: The department that helps customers with issues
vocabulary.example: Contact customer service for help.

vocabulary.meaning: When a product is unavailable because it has been sold out
vocabulary.example: Sorry, that item is out of stock.

vocabulary.meaning: Ordering a product before it is available
vocabulary.example: You can pre-order the new phone today.

vocabulary.meaning: A product made in small numbers for a short time
vocabulary.example: This is a limited edition sneaker.

vocabulary.meaning: A label attached to a product showing its price
vocabulary.example: Check the price tag before adding to your cart.

vocabulary.meaning: A passage between rows of shelves in a supermarket
vocabulary.translation: lo di giua cac ke hang
vocabulary.example: The cereal is in aisle 3.

vocabulary.meaning: A large building with many stores inside
vocabulary.example: Let's meet at the shopping mall this afternoon.

vocabulary.meaning: An unplanned purchase made on sudden desire
vocabulary.example: I made an impulse buy at the checkout.

vocabulary.meaning: A store's policy to match a competitor's lower price
vocabulary.translation: chinh sach ngang bang gia doi thu
vocabulary.example: We offer a price match guarantee.

vocabulary.meaning: Previously owned or used by someone else
vocabulary.example: I bought a second-hand bicycle.

vocabulary.meaning: Looking at goods in shop windows without buying
vocabulary.translation: di dao xem hang khong mua
vocabulary.example: I enjoy window shopping on weekends.

vocabulary.meaning: An expression of dissatisfaction about a product or service
vocabulary.example: I made a complaint about the damaged product.

vocabulary.meaning: Shopping as a way to improve one's mood
vocabulary.translation: mua sam de giai toa tam trang
vocabulary.example: She went for some retail therapy after a stressful week.

vocabulary.meaning: A hand-held basket for carrying items in a store
vocabulary.example: I grabbed a shopping basket at the entrance.

vocabulary.meaning: Checking prices from different shops before buying
vocabulary.example: Always do a price comparison before buying electronics.

vocabulary.meaning: A short-time sale offering big discounts
vocabulary.example: The flash sale lasted only two hours.

vocabulary.meaning: Money returned when you pay more than the price
vocabulary.example: Here is your change — $5.50.

vocabulary.meaning: A card allowing you to buy goods and pay later
vocabulary.example: Can I pay by credit card?

vocabulary.meaning: A person who loves to shop excessively
vocabulary.example: She is a shopaholic — always buying something new.

vocabulary.meaning: The times when a store is open for business
vocabulary.example: Check the store's open hours before visiting.

vocabulary.meaning: A machine that lets you scan and pay for items yourself
vocabulary.example: I used the self-checkout to avoid the queue.

quiz.question: What is a reduction in the original price called?
Where do you try on clothes in a store?
What do you ask for when returning a defective item?
What is the person who handles payments called?
What do you use to carry items while shopping?
